title: amphicarpaea ferruginea var. bracteosa
intro: Amphicarpaea ferruginea var. bracteosa, also known as the joint-vine or hog-peanut, is a perennial climbing plant native to eastern North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiparasitic properties. The health benefits of Amphicarpaea ferruginea var. bracteosa include its ability to treat skin conditions, wounds, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as its use as a natural remedy for fever and rheumatism. Traditionally, herbal preparations of this plant have been made by infusing the roots, leaves, and seeds in water or alcohol to create teas, tinctures, and salves, which are used to treat various health conditions.
